spec.reasoning: Three safe anchor types are used. Lexical: renamed 'Efficiency amount' → 'Return Amount', 'End amount' → 'Year-End Balance', and 'Total ability' → 'Cumulative Wealth'. These columns are not referenced by their exact names in the instruction text (the instruction uses 'efficiency' as a general English word describing rates, not as a column reference, so the 'Efficiency' column itself is left unchanged; 'Deposit' and 'Year' also appear as common English words in the instruction so they are untouched). Sheet renamed from 'Sheet1' to 'WealthTracker'. Positional_title_row: a title row is inserted at row 1, pushing headers to row 2 and data to row 3+. The answer position shifts from H2:H40 to H3:H41. The instruction does not reference absolute row numbers so this is safe. Distractor_column: 'Adjusted Rate' is added after the last column — plausibly related to efficiency/inflation adjustments but clearly distinct from existing columns. None of these changes alter data values, formula logic, or task difficulty.
